site.title: syllogism.info
site.tagline: A map of arguments in analytic philosophy
site.description: Every premise is itself a claim. Click any premise to see the arguments for it, all the way down to the root premises.

psr.text: Every contingent fact has an explanation (the Principle of Sufficient Reason)

bccf.text: There is a Big Conjunctive Contingent Fact (BCCF): the conjunction of all contingent facts

bccf-needs-necessary.text: Any explanation of the BCCF must involve the causal activity of a necessary being

necessary-being.text: A necessary being whose activity explains all contingent facts exists

necessary-being-is-god.text: If a necessary being whose activity explains all contingent facts exists, then God exists

causal-principle.text: Whatever begins to exist has a cause of its beginning

universe-caused.text: The universe has a cause of its beginning

kalam-bridge.text: If the universe has a cause of its beginning, then a personal, transcendent creator exists

no-actual-infinite.text: An actually infinite number of things cannot exist

beginningless-past-infinite.text: A beginningless series of past events would be an actually infinite number of things

ft-data.text: The fundamental constants and quantities of our universe are fine-tuned for the existence of life

ft-improbable-naturalism.text: A life-permitting universe is very improbable given atheistic naturalism

ft-not-improbable-theism.text: A life-permitting universe is not improbable given theism

likelihood-principle.text: Evidence that is much more probable under one hypothesis than under a rival strongly favors that hypothesis (the likelihood principle)

gratuitous-evil.text: There exist instances of intense suffering which an omnipotent, omniscient being could have prevented without thereby losing some greater good or permitting some evil equally bad or worse

rowe-theological-premise.text: An omniscient, wholly good being would prevent the occurrence of any intense suffering it could, unless it could not do so without thereby losing some greater good or permitting some evil equally bad or worse

inscrutable-evils.text: There are evils — such as a fawn burning slowly to death in a remote forest fire — for which prolonged reflection reveals no outweighing good

noseeum-inference.text: If prolonged reflection reveals no outweighing good served by an evil, that is good (defeasible) evidence that there is none

loving-god-open.text: If a perfectly loving God exists, God is open to a personal relationship with every finite person capable of one

relationship-requires-belief.text: A person cannot be in a personal relationship with God without believing that God exists

loving-god-no-nonresistant-nonbelief.text: If a perfectly loving God exists, no capable person is a nonresistant nonbeliever

nonresistant-nonbelief.text: There are capable persons who are nonresistant nonbelievers

god-perfectly-loving.text: If God exists, God is perfectly loving

sensory-coherence.text: Our sensory experiences are richly detailed, coherent, and lawfully ordered

rwh-best-explanation.text: The real-world hypothesis explains the order and coherence of our experience better than any skeptical rival (dreams, demons, simulations)

ibe-principle.text: We are justified in believing the hypothesis that best explains our evidence
